rules.name: mTOR inhibition reduces senescence
rules.description: mTOR inhibition (rapamycin) promotes autophagy and reduces SASP

rules.name: AMPK activation mimics caloric restriction
rules.description: AMPK activation (metformin) improves nutrient sensing and autophagy

rules.name: NAD+ restoration improves mitochondrial function
rules.description: NAD+ precursors (NMN/NR) restore sirtuin activity and mitochondrial health

rules.name: Senolytics clear senescent cells
rules.description: Senolytic compounds (D+Q, Fisetin, Navitoclax) selectively kill senescent cells

rules.name: Partial epigenetic reprogramming reverses aging
rules.description: OSKM/OSK resets epigenetic clocks and hallmark scores toward youthful state

rules.name: SIRT6 overexpression improves genomic stability
rules.description: SIRT6 promotes DNA repair, suppresses NF-κB inflammation, maintains telomeres

rules.name: Telomerase extends telomeres
rules.description: TERT overexpression lengthens telomeres and delays replicative senescence

rules.name: Spermidine induces autophagy
rules.description: Spermidine promotes autophagy via eIF5A hypusination and TFEB activation

rules.name: FOXO3 activation improves stress resistance
rules.description: FOXO3 activates autophagy, DNA repair, and antioxidant programs

rules.name: Klotho suppresses aging pathways
rules.description: Klotho inhibits insulin/IGF-1 signaling, reduces oxidative stress

rules.name: NRF2 activation reduces oxidative damage
rules.description: NRF2/KEAP1 pathway activation improves antioxidant defense and proteostasis

rules.name: HDAC inhibition opens chromatin
rules.description: HDAC inhibitors increase histone acetylation, potentially rejuvenating epigenetic state

rules.name: p16 knockdown — senescence bypass with cancer risk
rules.description: Loss of p16 reduces senescence but is oncogenic

rules.name: p53 knockdown — apoptosis bypass with cancer risk
rules.description: Loss of p53 prevents apoptosis/senescence but is severely oncogenic

rules.name: TFAM overexpression improves mitochondrial biogenesis
rules.description: TFAM drives mtDNA replication and transcription, rescuing age-related decline

rules.name: αKG modulates epigenetic landscape via TET enzymes
rules.description: Alpha-ketoglutarate is a cofactor for TET demethylases, promoting DNA demethylation

rules.name: NF-κB inhibition reduces chronic inflammation
rules.description: Inhibition of NF-κB (e.g., via IKK inhibitors or parthenolide) suppresses pro-inflammatory cytokine production, reducing inflammaging and SASP-driven intercellular signaling disruption.

rules.name: JAK/STAT inhibition reduces SASP
rules.description: JAK1/2 inhibitors (e.g., ruxolitinib) block paracrine SASP signaling from senescent cells, attenuating tissue-wide inflammaging without directly clearing senescent cells.

rules.name: IL-6 neutralization reduces inflammaging
rules.description: Monoclonal antibody neutralization of IL-6 (tocilizumab) or its receptor suppresses the primary driver of systemic inflammaging, improving tissue homeostasis and reducing senescence reinforcement via paracrine loops.

rules.name: TFEB overexpression improves macroautophagy and proteostasis
rules.description: TFEB (Transcription Factor EB) is the master regulator of lysosomal biogenesis and autophagy gene expression. Overexpression enhances autophagic flux, clears aggregated proteins, and improves proteostasis.

rules.name: BECN1 overexpression promotes autophagy
rules.description: Beclin-1 (BECN1) is a core autophagy initiation factor. Overexpression restores age-related decline in autophagic flux, reduces protein aggregates, and extends healthspan in model organisms.

rules.name: Trehalose induces autophagy independently of mTOR
rules.description: Trehalose, a natural disaccharide, induces autophagy through an mTOR-independent mechanism (TFEB nuclear translocation). It clears misfolded proteins and aggregate-prone species, improving proteostasis.

rules.name: PGC1α/PPARGC1A overexpression drives mitochondrial biogenesis
rules.description: PGC-1α is the master co-activator of mitochondrial biogenesis. Overexpression increases mitochondrial mass, improves oxidative phosphorylation efficiency, and reduces ROS production in aged tissues.

rules.name: PINK1 overexpression enhances mitophagy and mitochondrial quality control
rules.description: PINK1 kinase recruits Parkin to damaged mitochondria, triggering mitophagy. Overexpression improves selective clearance of dysfunctional mitochondria, reducing ROS and improving overall mitochondrial network quality.

rules.name: MitoQ/SS-31 reduces mitochondrial ROS
rules.description: Mitochondria-targeted antioxidants (MitoQ, SS-31/elamipretide) accumulate in the mitochondrial matrix and inner membrane, quenching ROS at the source, preserving mtDNA integrity and membrane potential.

rules.name: Wnt pathway modulation supports stem cell maintenance
rules.description: Balanced Wnt signaling (neither constitutively active nor inactive) is required for stem cell self-renewal. Age-appropriate Wnt restoration reduces stem cell exhaustion and improves tissue regenerative capacity.

rules.name: GDF11 promotes stem cell and tissue rejuvenation
rules.description: GDF11 (Growth Differentiation Factor 11), a circulating TGF-β family member, declines with age. Restoration improves muscle stem cell function, cardiac hypertrophy reversal, and neurogenesis in aged animals.

rules.name: ATM activation improves genomic stability
rules.description: ATM kinase is the master sensor of DNA double-strand breaks. Pharmacological activation or expression restoration enhances DNA damage response, reduces mutation accumulation, and suppresses genomic instability in aged cells.

rules.name: PARP1 modulation enhances DNA repair
rules.description: PARP1 is a key enzyme in single-strand break repair. Careful modulation (activation at physiological levels) restores efficient DNA repair, while over-activation depletes NAD+. Net benefit at restoration doses is reduced genomic instability.

rules.name: SIRT1 activation improves nutrient sensing and epigenetic maintenance
rules.description: SIRT1 deacetylates histones and transcription factors (p53, FOXO, NF-κB), improving epigenetic fidelity and metabolic sensing. Activation by resveratrol or SRT2104 mimics caloric restriction benefits.

rules.name: SIRT3 overexpression improves mitochondrial deacetylation
rules.description: SIRT3 is the primary mitochondrial deacetylase. Overexpression reduces hyperacetylation of OXPHOS complexes and metabolic enzymes in aged mitochondria, improving ATP production and reducing ROS.

rules.name: SIRT7 maintains rDNA stability and nucleolar integrity
rules.description: SIRT7 deacetylates H3K18 at rDNA repeats and interacts with RNA Pol I, maintaining nucleolar integrity. Age-related SIRT7 decline leads to rDNA instability and increased epigenetic noise.

rules.name: RAS overexpression increases genomic instability (oncogenic)
rules.description: Constitutively active RAS (KRAS G12D, HRAS G12V) drives oncogene-induced senescence acutely but promotes genomic instability, aneuploidy, and malignant transformation in permissive contexts. Strongly oncogenic.

rules.name: AKT overexpression drives proliferation with cancer risk (oncogenic)
rules.description: Constitutively active AKT1/AKT2 overrides apoptotic checkpoints, promotes hyperproliferative signaling, suppresses FOXO activity, and elevates cancer risk across tissue types.

rules.name: BCL2 overexpression blocks apoptosis (oncogenic)
rules.description: BCL2 overexpression inhibits mitochondrial apoptosis, allowing survival of damaged and pre-malignant cells. While used therapeutically in B-cell lymphoma contexts, constitutive overexpression increases cancer risk and senescent cell burden by blocking clearance.

rules.name: Ketone body supplementation improves mitochondrial function and nutrient sensing
rules.description: Exogenous ketones (beta-hydroxybutyrate, BHB) or ketogenic diet provide an alternative fuel substrate, inhibit NLRP3 inflammasome, act as HDAC inhibitors via BHB-mediated histone modification, and improve mitochondrial efficiency.

rules.name: Taurine supplementation improves multiple hallmarks
rules.description: Taurine, a semi-essential sulfur amino acid, declines with age. Restoration improves mitochondrial function, reduces DNA damage, suppresses cellular senescence, and extends healthspan in multiple model organisms. Acts broadly across hallmarks.
